title: Magical Crusty Malaysian Bread Toast
description: A tangy and crisp Malaysian dish featuring crusty bread, perfect for a brunch mood, served in a fondue pot for a fun and interactive experience.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Slice the crusty bread into 1-inch thick slices and toast until lightly browned.
Marinate the tempeh in a mixture of soy sauce, lime juice, and chili flakes for at least 30 minutes.
Bake the marinated tempeh in the oven for 20 minutes, or until golden brown.
Heat the coconut oil in a small saucepan over medium heat.
Add the minced garlic to the saucepan and cook for 1 minute, or until fragrant.
Add the chopped scallions to the saucepan and cook for an additional 2 minutes.
Assemble the dish by placing the toasted bread slices in the fondue pot, followed by the baked tempeh and garlic-scallion mixture.
Sprinkle the sesame seeds and salt over the top of the dish.
